expectations.text: Extracts the real shared conventions present in at least three files: functions return a Result object ({ ok, value } / { ok, error }) instead of throwing, and use the structured logger (logger.warn with an event name and context object).
Documented patterns are evidence-based and cite the actual fixture files, not generic best-practice advice or libraries/patterns that do not appear in the source.

Produces an Avoid list grounded in the outlier (legacy_refund_service.js): a prescriptive rule against throwing raw errors across layer boundaries and against using console.log instead of the structured logger, framed as a rule rather than an inventory of offending files.

expectations.text: Identifies the recurring naming/structure convention seen in at least three files: one handler module per command, snake_case file names ending in _handler, each exporting a handle(cmd) function.
Conventions are grounded in the actual handler files rather than asserting generic Python or architectural advice not present in the fixture.
Builds an Avoid rule from the MiscHandler.py outlier — e.g. do not use CamelCase handler file names or a non-standard entrypoint (run) instead of handle — expressed as a prescriptive rule.
